title: Sandy
caption_full:
What draws human beings to the highest places on Earth? This July, Karwaan Baithak invites you to Where the Earth Ends: A Discourse on the Curious Case of 1924 Expedition of Mallory and Irvine, and the Human Desire to Climb Everest, a conversation with Professor Ajay Dandekar.
For nearly a century, one question has continued to haunt climbers, historians, and explorers alike: Did George Mallory and Andrew "Sandy" Irvine reach the summit of Mount Everest in 1924, twenty-nine years before Edmund Hillary and Tenzing Norgay? When Mallory's body was discovered in 1999, the mountain surrendered part of its secret—but Irvine, and the camera that may hold the answer, remain lost somewhere on Everest.
Drawing upon expedition diaries, historical research, photographs, and the remarkable story of the 1924 British Everest Expedition, Professor Ajay Dandekar will take us through one of the greatest detective stories in modern history, while reflecting on exploration, ambition, geography, history, and the philosophy of adventure.
📅 Sunday 19th July 2026
🕓 4:00 PM onwards
📍 Arthshila, New Delhi
Seats are limited, and registrations are now open. Link in bio.
Come join us for an evening that journeys from the archives to the Himalaya, and asks what it is that keeps human beings climbing toward horizons where maps end and imagination begins.

title: their final demonstrations
caption_full:
Join us in experiencing some of the best theatrical productions by child artists with Rang Amlan 2026 – Children’s Theatre Festival & Workshop.
Organised by the National School of Drama (NSD) in collaboration with Delhi Tourism and Transportation Development Corporation (DTTDC), the festival will open in 4 July at 6:00 PM at Satyagraha Mandap, Gandhi Smriti & Darshan Samiti, Rajghat, New Delhi, with the play “Shanti Ki Pukar” directed by Smt. Nisha Trivedi.
From 5 to 9 July, audiences can enjoy daily performances at 7:15 PM. The line-up includes “Premchand Ki Ladkiyan” directed by Sh. Jayant Rabha, “Bhootnagri” directed by Sh. Gulshan Walia, “Jake Jake Uri Jai Sarai” directed by Sh. Parag Sarmah, “Ribike, Hara Dadhi Wala Raja aur Vishal Bean Ka Paudha” directed by Sh. Vikrant Singh, and “Mobile-A?-Sur?” directed by Sh. Himmat Singh Negi.
Alongside these productions, 155 children are participating in intensive workshops, learning theatre skills and discovering the joy of stagecraft. The festival will conclude on 10 July at Abhimanch Auditorium, NSD, where the workshop groups will present their final demonstrations.
𝐕𝐞𝐧𝐮𝐞: Satyagraha Mandap, Rajghat, New Delhi
𝐍𝐞𝐚𝐫𝐞𝐬𝐭 𝐌𝐞𝐭𝐫𝐨: Delhi Gate
Entry is free.
@narendramodi @gssjodhpur @raoinderjitsingh @amritmahotsav @ministryofculturegoi @pibindia

title: Join us for a musical evening; “The Madness Has Begun! - The Bochinche Tour 2026”. Candeleros, a Madrid-based Spanish–South American sextet, presents a distinctive musical performance that bridges tradition and modernity. Their sound integrates Afro-Venezuelan and Caribbean rhythms with elements of psychedelia, electronic textures, and punk energy. Anchored by percussion, drum machines, guitars, and synthesizers, the ensemble reinterprets ancestral rhythms within a sophisticated contemporary framework, reflecting the multicultural vitality of 21st-century Madrid. An evening of compelling and immersive live music. Collab. Embassy of Spain.
caption_full:

title: We look forward to welcoming you there!
caption_full:
The Shifting of Silence marks a collaborative venture between Blueprint12 and Space118 Art Foundation (@space118_foundation) bringing together works of 32 emerging contemporary artists from across India.
A fundraising initiative, the proceeds from the exhibition will support the Space118 Fine Arts Grant which provides financial assistance, mentorship and professional development opportunities, continuing both Blueprint12’s and Space118 Art’s commitment of investing directly back into the arts ecosystem.
We look forward to welcoming you there!

On view until 2nd August, 2026
At, LTC, Bikaner House, New Delhi.

caption_full:

title: One Evening. One Celebration. One Musical Legacy.
venue: Stein Auditorium, India Habitat Centre, New Delhi
caption_full:
✨ One Evening. One Celebration. One Musical Legacy. ✨
With immense joy and gratitude, Saaz-O-Awaaz warmly invites you to GuruDiksha 2026—an unforgettable evening dedicated to the legendary Tabla Maestro, Ustad Rafiuddin Sabri Ji, celebrating the timeless bond of the Guru–Shishya Parampara and this is even more special as we are also celebrating our Dear Ustadji’s birthday also.
🎶 Featuring:
🥁 Mst. Abdul Razzaq – Tabla Solo (with Sh. Lalit Sisodia) @razzaqrafiuddinsabri2022
🎙️ Mohammad Amaan Khan – Vocal Recital (accompanied by Sh. Ujith Uday Kumar & Sh. Lalit Sisodia) @amaanmohd190 @ujith_udayofficial @lalit_sisodiaofficial
🎼 Grand Finale: Pt. Purbayan Chatterjee & Ustad Rafiuddin Sabri sharing the stage for the very first time!
@purbayanch @official_rafiuddinsabri
📍 Stein Auditorium, India Habitat Centre, New Delhi
📅 14th July 2026 | 🕖 7:00 PM
Poster Courtesy - @photowala_bro
Come celebrate music, tradition, and the birthday of a living legend. We look forward to welcoming you! 🙏🎶
.
.
.

title: The Other Side
caption_full:
/Triveni Gallery
THE OTHER SIDE
A Solo Exhibition by Ravjot Singh
Triveni Kala Sangam presents The Other Side, a solo exhibition by Ravjot Singh.
Exploring themes of memory, spirituality, and contemplation, the exhibition reflects on the relationship between the self and the vastness that lies beyond ordinary perception. The works invite viewers into a space of stillness, encouraging a slower way of looking where reflection, presence, and awareness become central to the experience.
In a world shaped by constant movement and excess, The Other Side offers an opportunity to pause, observe, and allow meaning to unfold through quiet contemplation.
On View: 12–20 July 2026
Timings: 11:00 AM – 8:00 PM
Venue: Triveni Gallery

title: Mst. Abdul Razzaq, son and disciple of Ustad Rafiuddin Sabri Ji, as
venue: Stein Auditorium, India Habitat Centre, New Delhi
caption_full:
The evening begins with the brilliance of young talent. ✨
We are delighted to present Mst. Abdul Razzaq, son and disciple of Ustad Rafiuddin Sabri Ji, as the first artist of the evening at GuruDiksha 2026.
With a legacy rooted in the rich tradition, Abdul Razzaq carries forward his Guru’s blessings through every stroke of the tabla. Accompanied by the accomplished Sh. Lalit Sisodia on harmonium, this recital promises an inspiring beginning to an unforgettable evening dedicated to our revered Guru.
📅 14th July 2026
🕖 7:00 PM
📍 Stein Auditorium, India Habitat Centre, New Delhi
Title Sponsor - IRFC (Indian Railway Finance Corporation) @irfc_ir
Gold sponsor - @oleeviagrameen
Supported by - @panickerstravelindia & @rababmusicals
Poster Courtesy - @photowala_bro
Join us in celebrating the timeless Guru–Shishya Parampara through the language of music.

title: Shridharani Gallery
caption_full:
/Shridharani Gallery
There Are Rivers in the Sky
A Solo Exhibition by Saruha Kilaru
Curated by Priyanshi S
Taking its title from There Are Rivers in the Sky, this exhibition explores water as memory, an enduring presence that carries traces of landscapes, ecologies, histories, and lived experience across time.
Working across glass, textiles, printmaking, sculpture, and installation, Saruha Kilaru's practice reflects on perception, materiality, and the interconnectedness of the natural world. Through delicate forms and immersive installations, the exhibition invites viewers to consider water not only as a subject, but as a way of thinking: fluid, relational, and constantly transforming.
Preview: Saturday, 11 July 2026 | 6:00 PM onwards
On View: 11–17 July 2026
Shridharani Gallery
Triveni Kala Sangam

title: EXHIBITION INVITATION
The Grand Finale awaits…
venue: Stein Auditorium, India Habitat Centre, Lodhi Road, New Delhi
caption_full:
The Grand Finale awaits… ✨🎶
As the curtains draw closer to an unforgettable evening, GuruDiksha 2026 proudly presents its Grand Finale—a historic confluence of two stalwarts of Indian Classical Music.
Celebrated Sitar Maestro Pt. Purbayan Chatterjee @purbayanch
Renowned Tabla Maestro Ustad Rafiuddin Sabri @official_rafiuddinsabri
Sharing the stage together for the very first time.
An extraordinary musical dialogue where the lyrical elegance of the sitar meets the majestic brilliance of the tabla, creating a once-in-a-lifetime experience for every rasika. This landmark recital is a heartfelt offering to the spirit of the Guru–Shishya Parampara and a fitting culmination to an evening dedicated to Ustad Rafiuddin Sabri Ji.
Don’t miss this rare and historic collaboration.
📅 14th July 2026
🕖 7:00 PM onwards
📍 Stein Auditorium, India Habitat Centre, Lodhi Road, New Delhi
Be there to witness history in the making.

title: Ticket link in bio.
caption_full:
Join us for an evening of theatre; “Bunkar… Stories Woven Anew” (Hindi/ 75 mins) Directed & Performed by Danish Iqbal. Bunkar is an immersive blend of storytelling, theatre, and live music that brings everyday experiences to life through Cycle Kahani by Kunal Singh, explores childhood, friendship, love, and life’s bittersweet moments with warmth, humour, and nostalgia; Hero Ek Love Story by Ravi Bule, a tale of a charming young romantic, lost in daydreams of his neighbour, is jolted back to reality when her shocking revelation shatters his world. Prod. Rangsaaz Theatre Group.
Tickets at Rs.500, Rs.350 and Rs.200 available online on bookmyshow.com and at the Programmes desk, IHC.

title: a solo exhibition of works by Afzal Pathan (1936–2000), one of
venue: Venue: Shridharani Gallery, Triveni Kala Sangam, New Delhi
caption_full:
Afzal Pathan: Ek Bahta Dariya
Raza Foundation, in collaboration with Triveni Kala Sangam, presents a solo exhibition of works by Afzal Pathan (1936–2000), one of the most distinctive voices in Indian abstract art.
Known for his luminous forms, flowing compositions, and remarkable technical mastery, Pathan created a visual language that was both deeply personal and universally resonant. His paintings invite viewers into a world of movement, rhythm, and quiet contemplation.
Curated by Akhilesh, the exhibition offers an opportunity to revisit the legacy of an artist whose work continues to inspire generations of painters and art lovers.
Opening: 30 June 2026, 6:30 PM
Exhibition Dates: 30 June – 10 July 2026
Venue: Shridharani Gallery, Triveni Kala Sangam, New Delhi
Gallery Hours: 11:00 AM – 8:00 PM

title: Join us for an evening of theatre; “Tragedy Mein Comedy” (Hindi/ 80 mins) Wri. & Dir. Varun Sharma. The plot revolves around a murder in Hasija mansion followed by a series of events which questions the authenticity of the situation, the play takes a humorous turn when everyone gets involved and is suspected for the murder. With each proceeding, the case gets more and more complicated and finally gets concluded on an unexpected note. Prod. Theatreleela Acting Studio.

title: NEO, URBAN, AVATAR: A Mythopoesis of the Contemporary Times-II
caption_full:
Exhibition Alert 🔔
𝐍𝐄𝐎, 𝐔𝐑𝐁𝐀𝐍, 𝐀𝐕𝐀𝐓𝐀𝐑: 𝐀 𝐌𝐲𝐭𝐡𝐨𝐩𝐨𝐞𝐬𝐢𝐬 𝐨𝐟 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐂𝐨𝐧𝐭𝐞𝐦𝐩𝐨𝐫𝐚𝐫𝐲 𝐓𝐢𝐦𝐞𝐬-𝐈𝐈
𝐀𝐫𝐭 𝐰𝐨𝐫𝐤𝐬 𝐛𝐲 𝐁𝐢𝐫𝐞𝐧𝐝𝐫𝐚 𝐏𝐚𝐧𝐢 𝐍𝐄𝐎, 𝐔𝐑𝐁𝐀𝐍, 𝐀𝐕𝐀𝐓𝐀𝐑: 𝐀 𝐌𝐲𝐭𝐡𝐨𝐩𝐨𝐞𝐬𝐢𝐬 𝐨𝐟 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐂𝐨𝐧𝐭𝐞𝐦𝐩𝐨𝐫𝐚𝐫𝐲 𝐓𝐢𝐦𝐞𝐬-𝐈𝐈
𝐀𝐫𝐭 𝐰𝐨𝐫𝐤𝐬 𝐛𝐲 𝐁𝐢𝐫𝐞𝐧𝐝𝐫𝐚 𝐏𝐚𝐧𝐢
The exhibition presents a body of drawing-based paintings that explore the fragmented realities of contemporary urban existence. Rooted in Indian aesthetic traditions yet shaped by a globalized and technologized visual culture, the works weave together mythology, memory, satire, consumerism and environmental traces into an evolving contemporary mythscape.
𝐓𝐡𝐞 𝐞𝐱𝐡𝐢𝐛𝐢𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧 𝐰𝐢𝐥𝐥 𝐛𝐞 𝐢𝐧𝐚𝐮𝐠𝐮𝐫𝐚𝐭𝐞𝐝 𝐨𝐧 𝐓𝐮𝐞𝐬𝐝𝐚𝐲, 𝟑𝟎 𝐉𝐮𝐧𝐞’𝟐𝟔 𝐚𝐭 𝟏𝟕:𝟑𝟎 𝐡𝐫𝐬
𝐄𝐱𝐡𝐢𝐛𝐢𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧 𝐰𝐚𝐥𝐤𝐭𝐡𝐫𝐨𝐮𝐠𝐡𝐬 𝐝𝐚𝐢𝐥𝐲 𝐛𝐲 𝐭𝐡𝐞 𝐚𝐫𝐭𝐢𝐬𝐭 𝐚𝐭 𝟏𝟕:𝟑𝟎 𝐡𝐫𝐬
📆 𝟎𝟏 -𝟏𝟒 𝐉𝐮𝐥 𝟐𝟎𝟐𝟔,
⏰ 𝟏𝟏:𝟎𝟎 𝐀𝐌-𝟎𝟕:𝟎𝟎 𝐏𝐌
📍𝐀𝐫𝐭 𝐆𝐚𝐥𝐥𝐞𝐫𝐲, 𝐊𝐚𝐦𝐚𝐥𝐚𝐝𝐞𝐯𝐢 𝐂𝐨𝐦𝐩𝐥𝐞𝐱, 𝐈𝐈𝐂

title: Khoyi Hui Aawazein – Saptak:
caption_full:
Khoyi Hui Aawazein – Saptak:
The Raza Foundation, in collaboration with the India International Centre, invites you to a special screening of poetry readings by seven distinguished Hindi poets whose words continue to illuminate our literary imagination:
Kirti Chaudhary, Shriram Verma, Chandrakant Devtale, Bhagwat Rawat, Jyotsna Milan, Vinod Kumar Shukla and Mangalesh Dabral
An evening to listen, remember, and reconnect with the enduring power of poetry.
Monday, 6 July 2026
6:30 pm
C. D. Deshmukh Auditorium
India International Centre
New Delhi
We look forward to your presence.

title: आशा है आप सभी से 17 जुलाई को दिल्ली के कमानी ऑडिटोरियम में भेंट होगी।
caption_full: आशा है आप सभी से 17 जुलाई को दिल्ली के कमानी ऑडिटोरियम में भेंट होगी। 💐

title: Where Cinema Meets Cultural Heritage.
caption_full:
Where Cinema Meets Cultural Heritage. ✨
The Art Grandeur proudly joins hands with the Indira Gandhi National Centre for the Arts (IGNCA) to celebrate India’s rich artistic legacy at Film Expo India 2026. Together, we bridge cinema and culture, preserving traditions while inspiring creative futures.
📍 Bharat Mandapam, ITPO, New Delhi
📅 26–30 August 2026
